rulings.Case Title: HUDSON MOORE JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. FDCC satisfied its burden of production on the issue whether Dinwiddie exposed plaintiff to any asbestos by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether the joint compound attributable to Dinwiddie to which plaintiff was exposed contained asbestos. Charles Ay's declaration lacks foundation for his opinions on this issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CRULL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. DEFENDANT NATIONAL STEEL AND SHIPBUILDING COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN CARVETH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Cummins Engine Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Cummins satisfied its burden of production on the issue of asbestos content by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff's responses did not include sufficient evidence such that a reasonable juror could conclude that the gaskets attributable to Cummins contained asbestos. Kenneth Cohen's declaration does not provide a sufficient foundation for his conclusion that almost all automotive gaskets manufactured from the 1950s until the late 1980s contained asbestos. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY WHETMAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's motion for summary adjudication as to negligence and strict liability causes of action (issues 1 and 2) is denied. Union Carbide did not present any evidence that: 1) it was reasonable for Union Carbide to rely on Hamilton to warn plaintiff, 2) Union Carbide knew of Hamilton's level of sophistication, or 3) plaintiff was a sophisticated user of asbestos-containing products. (Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793). Consequently, whether called a sophisticated user or a sophisticated intermediary defense, the defense has not been shown to apply here as a matter of law. Union Carbide's motion as to false representation and punitive damages (issues 3 and 4) is moot as those claims have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY GEORGE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Amcord Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Amcord failed to attach the necessary evidence to support its statute of limitations affirmative defense. None of the pages of plaintiff's deposition testimony cited in the separate statement under the heading "All of Plaintiff Randy George's Causes of Action Against Amcord, Inc. Are Time Barred" were provided. Amcord failed to satisfy its burden of production on the issue of causation since plaintiff's discovery responses were not factually devoid. Plaintiff testified at his deposition that he worked with Riverside Gun Plastic Cement. Amcord's interrogatory responses state that Riverside Gun Plastic Cement contained asbestos during the period plaintiff contends that he was exposed to it. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY GEORGE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant CalPortland Company (FORMERLY KNOWN AS CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT COMPANY)'s motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether Mr. George's shortness of breath and other health problems at the time of his retirement were caused by exposure to asbestos. Mr George's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos containing Colton Gun Plastic Cement while he was employed by Edward Sugrhue. CalPortland's D'Amico objections are overruled =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BAKER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether Mr. Baker was exposed to asbestos-containing packing and gaskets installed on new Gardner Denver pumps. Gardner Denver's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues because Gardner Denver did not separately identify each cause of action or claim in its separate statement. (CRC 3.1350.) = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SARVER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant American Optical Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff raised a triable issue regarding his exposure to asbestos-containing mitts attributable to American Optical. The D'Amico objections are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW MCKINES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant Fluor Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff's objection as to the timeliness of the motion is sustained. The motion was served on plaintiff less than 75 days before the hearing. This court lacks the authority to shorten the minimum notice period for hearing a motion for summary judgment in the absence of both parties' consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 763.) =(220/HEK)
